as for my order the americano was perfect starbucks americano and also had a cheese pastry that was really nice.
i just wanted to give this shout out to aylin and amanda for their excellent service and customer experience , it just doesnt get much better, so thank you

review: i've been a loyal starbucks customer at this location for years. while i appreciate the generally friendly staff, i've frequently experienced order errors. in particular, i've had negative interactions with a specific employee (a blond woman) who has been dismissive when mistakes are pointed out. i recommend double-checking your order, especially if she is preparing it. otherwise, the rest of the team is great!

author: Hazel Jasper
review:
came to the till, one member of staff just standing on their mobile ignoring customers.  the rest of the staff all laughing and joking and mucking around behind the bar. a different staff member then came over, said hello and then proceeded to look away and stare into the ether not listening to our order.
i then repeat the order and ask for oat milk, as both me and my partner cannot have dairy. she puts the other through, and we take our drinks and leave. about 10 mins later we realise we have drank half and both are dairy… causing us both to be unwell.
the staff need to be spoken to, they were incredibly unprofessional and they are lucky it was not a milk allergy because it could have killed someone…. disappointed to say the least.

author: Antonio Giuliani
review:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
caffeine stop in style in london!
i stopped here while i was spinning like a top between oxford street and the thousand shops: hungry, thirsty and destroyed feet! but as soon as i entered this starbucks... magic! smell of coffee, chill atmosphere, blessed air conditioning.

the staff is super friendly (a barista even made me a little heart with the cappuccino foam, i swear!), the banana bread was so good that i seriously thought about getting a second one just for science.

perfect place to recharge your batteries - your phone and your brain. if you are around london and you need a tasty break with a pinch of pampering, this is your place.

ps: clean bathroom, which is rare. an extra point!

author: Cypher Muse
review:
i am extremely disappointed with my recent experience at this starbucks location. the person serving us, minche,
was shockingly rude and unprofessional. she displayed outright hostility, spoke to us with an aggressive attitude, and even swore at both me and my partner.

to make matters worse, she mocked our accent by making faces and gestures that were completely unacceptable. as someone who manages a restaurant myself, i know that this type of behavior reflects terribly on any business. i felt humiliated and disrespected in front of other customers.

this level of conduct is absolutely unacceptable in any service environment. i strongly believe minche should be held accountable for her actions and should be terminated immediately. no customer deserves to be treated this way.

i will not be returning to this location and will be sharing this experience with others to prevent them from going through similar mistreatment.

which means it’s free, which means you can have what quantity you want without paying because there’s no limit

author: Viola Chen
review:
starbucks in london is quite clean and bright, and the dining environment is much cleaner and more comfortable than local coffee shops (pret & noxy). the starbucks we visited near kcl and lse was packed with students, so there was a line around lunchtime.

during my 23-day stay in london, i frequented this cafe the most. i ordered two dishes, both of which were delicious even heated up. unfortunately, the café latte was a bit bitter. i wonder if it was the milk? the milk i struggled with most in london is the lack of flavor and flavor. (a bit strange 🤔) i later tried a couple of different brands, and things got better.

review:
if i could give this place 0 stars i would. apparently nhs workers could get a free drink today as a thank you but here it was more like we were an inconvenience. any drink was  apparently allowed in the offer but not here the only available drinks were "latte, americano and cappuccino" when asked why we could not pick any drink as advertised we were informed "well we wouldn't make any money then". my colleague asked for a hot chocolate but was again met with the above response yet when another colleague went a few hours later a hot chocolate was an available drink! c'mon! i have been in here before and the staff were again rude. i will not attend again.

team costa.

author: Lisa Lightfoot
review: i usually detest going out for a coffee as i find the performance of being a specialised wheelchair user, manoeuvring doors, tables, queues etc really difficult to manage. visiting the starbucks at the m.r.i however was a trip i will gladly repeat. my daughter and i were greeted at the door by a very happy and friendly lady who opened the door and greeted us both with a compliment, then as we queued she came over to us and asked very politely and discreetly if we were staying in or taking out and when we replied with staying in she then said she would sort out a table for me, which was then sorted to suit my needs. now she wasn’t the only member of staff who helped us out that day, i noted that every member of staff was really polite, helpful and genuinely engaged with the customer. usually if someone is trying to be as helpful as they were it can come over as a pitying or patronising manner but not in this store. at the end of the visit i asked one of the baristas if he knew the name of the lady who had held the door open and helped me out to which i was told, ‘oh you mean elaine, yes she’s our manager and she’s great!’ so from beginning to end we had great service, great coffee and people who genuinely seemed to enjoy working there as a team. elaine if you read this you and your team made my day, so thank you.

author: vietlinhdayne
review:
i carried a pret cup, walked into this starbucks and just ordered a large vanilla matcha latte at this starbucks.

it’s not my first time here but it is definitely my last time being a starbucks customer.

all the staff (the staff who took my order & and the staff who made the latte were decent and lovely) expected one.

there was a staff and who was not wearing any uniform or nametag so i didn’t know what her position there for now i called her a.

i asked for a cup holder cause i can’t carry two cups a once. the other staff was making another drink so he asked a to bring me a cup holder. a said (quote on quote): “no, the other one you didn’t buy from here so i can’t give it to you”. i was silenced for a moment because i didn’t know what to say and she continued: “can’t sorry”.

to that woman a who treated me so badly today:

i do not know what happened to you that makes you a grumpy person like today, but i hope everything is going to be fine for you, madam.

it’s just a cup holder and if i had asked for an ice cup you could have given me the cup holder already, but i didn’t. i chose to leave. arguing with a person who thinks that i took advantage of the store by asking for one paper cup holder would just make me more miserable. if i learned that buying giving me a cup holder because i carried both pret & starbucks cup got you into trouble, i wouldn’t ask for it but we all knoe that you are not into any trouble by giving me just a paper cup holder.

i don’t know if you treated me like that because of my look, or my background,  but i don’t think you would done that with a local.

i think that you found yourself lucky by bullying me with that attitude. because it would not end up like that with another single person.

for those who read this and think: “it’s just a cup holder get over it” then exactly! it’s just a cup holder how can one be so disrespectful and unpleasant attitude?

even though i have a pretty monthly subscription, i still come to starbucks and buy drinks. but from now on i don’t think that will be the case anymore.

for anyone craving for good matcha latte and a nice atmosphere cafe at cheaper prices, i’d recommend federal which is also based in oxford road.

starbucks will still be my favourite brand afterwards, but you madam a, i suggest you get rid of that way of treating people thinking you are solving a problem.

being flexible and treating people with love is how we work in this industry. you might change the place you work for but if that attitude remains and runs deep, i am sure starbucks will not just lose 1 customer.

for the other staff that stay silenced the whole time. i think you should have said something… but bottomline i understand it’s not your fault and you are buying and making drinks.

author: Ander L
review:
it was raining in manchester so i went to this particular starbucks attracted by the “more seating upstairs” sign. i thought it would be good to have a coffee relaxed while the rain stopped.

ordered my drink, specifically asked for it “to stay”, meaning that i wanted actual cup, not a paper cup. this was ignored. the staff couldn’t care less, you could tell by their attitude.

grabbed my drink and i was about to go upstairs when i noticed that the access to the stairs was blocked. this was due to upstairs being closed. the person at the till could have warn me after i asked for my drink “to stay”, but as i said, they couldn’t care less.

there were just two tables downstairs and these were occupied. so i basically had to leave.

awful experience. i usually avoid starbucks and stick to independent coffee shops. this was a good reminder to myself on why.

on another note, every single time i order a cortado at starbucks i get something different. and this has been my experience not just in the uk but also in different countries, especially at airports. this time wasn’t an exception.

i visited the starbucks at television house in manchester and really liked the place. it’s in a convenient city centre location, easy to reach and perfect for a quick coffee break or to relax for a while. the café was clean, comfortable, and well-maintained. staff were friendly and polite, and my order was served quickly.

the atmosphere was pleasant — nice background music, good lighting, and a calm vibe even though it’s usually quite busy during rush hours. there’s free wi-fi and enough seating for short stays, though it can get crowded at times.

overall, a nice spot for a coffee in the heart of manchester. i’d rate it 4 out of 5 for its service, cleanliness, and location.

temperature and he said yeah abruptly. told him
the coffee was meant to be warm to which he threw the coffee down the sink and began making another coffee. didn’t say a word to me or apologised for the mix up just mumbled under his breath. even when handing the coffee he just made it and put it there. i had to ask if that was my coffee. didn’t look the part and definitely doesn’t have customer service.

author: Pasha Melli
review:
extremely hot coffee and poor customer service

i visited the starbucks in whitworth street west and had an unpleasant experience that warrants a one-star rating. i ordered my usual coffee, but once again, it was served far too hot, making it impossible to drink. this is not the first time this has happened with the same barista ( chad?). when i politely pointed out that the coffee was excessively hot, the barista's solution was to pour it into another cup, creating a double layer. this did nothing to solve the actual problem; the coffee remained scalding and undrinkable.

to make matters worse, when i expressed my dissatisfaction, i was met with an unprofessional and dismissive attitude. instead of addressing the issue or offering to remake the drink at a more reasonable temperature, i was told that my feedback was unwelcome.

this level of customer service is unacceptable, and i hope the management takes note of this incident. serving coffee at a dangerously high temperature and then refusing to handle customer complaints properly is not what i expect from starbucks.

author: Adithya
review:
from a person who basically gets starbucks every other day, i definitely wouldn't visit this place despite it being 2 mins away from my house.

ordered an "iced brown sugar oat shaken espresso with less ice and hazelnuts syrup" – my standard order ever since oat shaken espresso was introduced a few years back.

the barista messed up the order, putting too much ice in it. i did contemplate asking them about it as i'm not that confrontational, and even walked back briefly.

but since it was a cold december day, i politely reminded them that it had too much ice in it while the order mentioned little ice in it.

the barista to my shock was very much annoyed with this simple request and just poured my coffee into a new cup and gave it back to me. which wasn't even half the size of the "tall drink" that i ordered. (minus the syrup which stayed in the old cup).

i mean, the whole point of ordering oat shaken espresso is that it's shaken and not mixed like a bad looking cold nescafe. forcing myself, i asked them again if this is what i'd be getting. the annoyed barista, snapped back saying this is what i get for ordering without ice. and if i wanted more i should've ordered with ice.

yeah cool, if i wanted that attitude and this drink, i would've made myself a nescafe at home. i just don't understand how every other starbucks has been making it right for the past 3 years.

i mean if this can be called a "tall oat 'shaken' espresso", what's stopping us from calling the earth flat huh? :) it was 5 pm on a saturday so, it wasn't even a busy time. (maybe for bunny's next door but definitely not here.)

author: J
review:
one of the worst starbucks experiences i have had in a long time. the staff during a busy morning are under pressure and because of this do not read the drinks orders correctly.

admittedly my order is complex but very few times does it ever go wrong - until this fateful morning. it had to be remade twice and even on the 3rd time it still wasn't as i wanted but i had to go to a meeting (i am not from this city so i really needed things to be right first time on my business trip).

each time i tried to get my drink re-made, i was made to feel it was my mistake. i asked for a chai latte with espresso shots and when there were none i was told "the chai latte doesn't have coffee in it" until i pointed to the label which clearly showed i asked for it to be added. i also mentioned it is supposed to be wet but when i sent it back a second time i was told "i have used a spoon to take the foam off" despite it being a good inch of foam on top.

the attitude is not great and the girls spend the whole time chatting about how they wanted extra shifts. master the basics first and they may get more shifts in future.

author: Ahmed Alattar
review:
i’ve been a loyal starbucks customer for years, even when others decided to boycott the brand. unfortunately, my recent experiences at the birmingham new street branch have left me quite disappointed due to the behavior of one of the employees, afyia.

on multiple occasions, i’ve noticed that she rarely smiles or makes an effort to be welcoming. it often feels like customers are not valued when she’s around. today was the tipping point. she came outside to collect chairs and tables, but instead of politely asking customers to move, she raised her voice and demanded, “excuse me, we’re going to take the chairs inside now!” the tone was aggressive and commanding, which made me and others feel extremely uncomfortable.

what bothered me most was that there were plenty of other chairs available that could have been taken first, but she felt the need to target a few customers and rush them in such an impolite way. there was no reason for this kind of behavior. basic courtesy goes a long way, and it’s disheartening to see this lack of professionalism, especially at a place like starbucks where customer service should be a priority.

great coffee is available everywhere these days, but customer service is what sets businesses apart. i hope starbucks addresses this issue at the birmingham new street branch, as it’s not the experience i expect from a brand i’ve been loyal to for years.

author: Mr Saturn
review:
usually have no problem here except today, with one of the staff.

i think her name was henry (black woman with long blonde braids) , i’m unsure why she has a job as a customer sever when she clearly hates it. makes no effort smile or to be welcoming speaks in the most monotone voice, after i informed her that the toilet had no toilet paper, she lethargically said “i will fix it” after asking when since i needed the toilet she said “now”, she of course did not put in any toilet paper.

unsure why she was being so rude, but i was nothing but respectful to her.

i then asked for a manager and she said there’s no managers on site, which is hard to believe.

unfortunate how one bad apple can ruin the entire pie.

first time i’ve written a review about bad customer service but here we are, managers need a talk with her.

other than that it’s usually a decent place to get work done

author: Zara
review:
i recently visited starbucks and ordered my usual coffee and some food. i’m quite an anxious person, especially around people, so even simple things like paying can make me nervous. when i was entering my card pin, i somehow mistyped it two or three times, and i started to feel really embarrassed because there was a long queue behind me.

but one of the staff members. i believe her name was afia, a lovely girl wearing a black scarf and black uniform was so kind and patient with me. she calmly told me, “it’s okay, take your time”when my card didn’t work the third time, i said i didn’t know what went wrong and was about to leave, but she said to me “it’s fine, it’s on us today.”
that small act of kindness honestly made my day. in a world where kindness feels rare, she showed genuine compassion and understanding when she didn’t have to. she could’ve easily just refused to serve me, but instead, she went out of her way to make sure i felt cared for.
after finishing my drink, i went back to thank her personally and wished her all the best in life. i truly pray she earns everything good happiness, success, and prosperity and a lot of money because she deserves it. god bless her. ❤️

author: rabar omer
review:
went into starbucks in birmingham city centre around 9:30, and honestly, it was one of the worst customer service experiences i’ve had. i stood there waiting — no one in front or behind me — and the staff member was right there, having just served other people. she clearly saw me standing there but didn’t say a single word, not even “i’ll be with you in a minute.”
after waiting for over a minute, she told me i could just order it myself on the ipad. like, really? what’s the point of having staff if customers are just supposed to serve themselves? i came to be served, not to do the job myself.

in the end, i walked out and went to 200 degrees coffee instead and the difference was unreal. friendly staff, amazing service, and way better coffee. starbucks seriously needs to train their staff on basic customer service, because right now, it’s just embarrassing.

definitely not going back there again.

then, after finishing my drink, i collected mine and someone else’s rubbish and brought it to the counter due to there not being a bin. when i asked, she became immediately frustrated and responded with “just put it on the work top” as if i done something wrong. and began shaking her head in disapproval of me.
while this is my first poor experience of this branch, this has made me feel like i will avoid further visits, which is disappointing due to the convenient location.
she probably had a bad day. but i think it is important that staff understand the effect poor service has. especially on a company like starbucks.

author: Oluwafemi Olajide
review:
i stepped into starbucks and immediately, the atmosphere felt like a warm embrace. the staff, wearing smiles as their uniform, made me feel like a cherished guest. from the very moment i walked in, i knew this was going to be a delightful experience.

let me tell you about the almond croissant – a heavenly creation with a flaky crust that seemed to melt in my mouth. the almond cream filling was like a secret treasure, rich and velvety. this wasn't just a pastry; it was a work of art, carefully crafted to perfection.

but the surprises didn't stop there. the banana caramel muffin was a symphony of flavors – sweet banana dancing with luscious caramel. and that topping? it wasn't just a topping; it was like a delicious crown, adding a crunch that was incredibly satisfying. believe me, this treat is a journey you won't want to miss.

then, there was the blueberry muffin – a burst of tangy and sweet berries in every bite. the sugar-crusted top was like a sweet secret waiting to be discovered with every mouthful. i couldn't help but close my eyes and savor the blissful moment.

the pain au chocolat was a rendezvous with sophistication. with each bite, it was like i was transported to a charming french café. the fusion of flavors was like a dance of elegance and comfort in my mouth.

and let's not forget the chocolate twist – it was like a playful game of hide and seek with chocolate surprises. every twist revealed a burst of cocoa goodness, and it was perfect for anyone with a love affair with chocolate.

so, here's my advice: plan a visit to starbucks, indulge in these delightful creations, and then come back to thank me for introducing you to this world of taste and wonder. your taste buds will thank you!

review:
ordered on the app and waited a few minutes before going to collect my order. there were two women just chatting away and she hadn’t even seen the order. bearing in mind they were absolutely free and had no other customers around. she said she’d prepare the order and give me a shout. after waiting 5-6 minutes i went back to find my order sitting on a tray on the table. the strawberry and cream frappe was all blended and had no cream on top - i hadn’t specified no whipped cream. it was absolutely nothing like what it should have been. all i could taste is the ice. i requested 10 pumps of strawberry sauce and it probably had around 5. also, the tables were absolutely filthy. i was sat there around 45 minutes and not once did they come and clear up the tables or remove empty trays/cups.

author: Anisa Nisar
review: was a little busy when i came into the store. i was unsure if i could get warm milk for my baby, so while joining the queue i asked a staff member who was cleaning. she was very polite and told me i could order it from her, and jump the queue instead of waiting. i declined and thanked her for the offer. the queue moved fast and before i knew it i was at the till. i was able to get full fat milk for my baby. they also didn't charge me, which was great!

author: Olivia Graham
review: i don’t like to leave bad reviews but the behaviour from hanah was very unnecessary and unprofessional. hanah was incredibly rude, showing no effort to greet us or take our order. it was quite loud inside and she very quietly said hi so we weren’t sure if she was even talking to us. we started to order however she made no move to go towards the till so we felt we could not speak to her as she was possibly busy. she stared at us with a far from kind expression. when she did finally start taking our order, we felt rushed and ignored as she acknowledged nothing we said and then very rudely sighing. my friend did not get the chance to ask for oat milk as we were hurried along to wait at the end of the counter. my friend then quickly apologised and asked if they could make her drink with oat milk possibly due to her intolerance. she was very apologetic that she had forgotten to ask but our drinks had not even started being made yet, hana rudely picked up our order sticker and spoke to her unkindly, my drink was then made, there’s no complaint about the drink as it was good. however my friends drink came with no oat milk and regular whipped cream, when she questioned if it was made with oat milk as there was nothing to say oat on the sticker, suraya and hanah spoke about the order as if my friend was an inconvenience. my friend never spoke rudely or even showed any upset however received nothing but rudeness back. we understand the staff are busy but it does not give the right to be rude to your customers. my friend asked for the order to be remade as she couldn’t have the regular cream as well and it was once again made to be an inconvenience and they even questioned her on if she had tried it and liked it.

author: Barry James
review:
i come here regularly. i like coming here as it is open late, closing at 9pm.

i noticed that the toilet downstairs had been closed off, leaving the disabled toilet for the entire pretty full cafe. this was about 5:45pm.

after few hot chocolates, i needed the loo. the disabled toilets were busy so i asked for the code from the staff, to be told that it was closed for cleaning and that i would need to use the disabled toilet. i advised the young lady that it was in use, i was told told with a shrug  that i would "just have to wait."

as someone who has quite severe... ahem,  digestive issues, i was too embarressed to make a big deal out of it. the young lady in question has no idea how close it came to them having a much more serious cleaning issue as i waited. the toilet was not opened again.

i have worked in hospitality extensively from uk to new zealand and i have never spoke  to any customer the way i was spoke n to today. i have never given an all 1 star review of a a cafe ever. all because the manager (or whoever) took the decision to close a legally required facility 3 hours early.

i'm not sure i'll be back.

author: Allan
review:
its not the plush interior, the ultra cool crowd, the art work adorning the walls (sorry planet hollywood/hard rock - that's "no bueno") but you could be forgiven for thinking so - nor is the speed or effortlessness with which you are served - it is simply the nutty biscuity taste of a damn good mug of coffee - that reason alone should be enough to get you to visit/revisit starbucks.
hot drinks for cold days, cold drinks for hot days, what more can you expect from a starbucks?!
you come here because you belong to the coffee shop generation - you come here because it's a chilling and killing zone (chilling out and killing time) - you come here because it's a refuge from the world war z crowds outside, but most important of all - you here because its that ibiza groove .   it's not "just another coffee shop - it's starbucks". nuff said bro

author: Jaki
review: i collected the wrong drink today (as i thought i was next in line) which was also given to me by a member of staff. when the baristas realised the mix up, i went down to them and asked if mine was the wrong drink and expressed my confusion. the staff member replied something along the lines of “the macchiato for josh? yeah”, in probably the cheekiest tone i’ve ever heard in hospitality - in my life. it was clear that this staff member felt hate in that moment - probably for herself too, as she also gave me the drink. i was very embarrassed as this conversation was loud and i offered “would you like me to pay for that?”, to which she said a stern “no”. she then (in a voice loud enough for other customers to hear) told me that there was “a tonne of dairy in that drink you just had”, i advised her that i was not intolerant. i found myself apologising and saying “sorry… that was just a mix up” - an understanding which i truly felt was absent in her own response (representing starbucks, don’t forget). in a fast paced and loud environment, you should not be able to place such angry-blame on to a customer who simply made an error while collecting their drink. change careers if you feel the need, as it is not okay to have customers leaving wishing that they had never set foot in there (and will never again).

review: staff is nice enough, but the place has really gone downhill since the refurbishment. the queuing system is confusing and i don't like how everyone is now corralled into one big (noisy) room upstairs. it was much nicer with downstairs seating.

author: Simon Champa
review:
i've updated my review since it reopened after the badly thought out refurbishment.

things i like

this is the only place on byres road to get a venti (huge) latte.

very central location - right next to the underground.

the staff are nice and friendly

things i don't like:

the coffee has a bit less twang or flavour than some of the other places i tried

the new refurbishment (while needed), has clearly been done by people who don't understand what a coffee shop is for.  the flow for queueing is now awful.  they eliminated a lot of downstairs seating which makes the place look sterile and corporate.

review: visited this shop today as we had hospital appointments in bristol. had to ask for it to be topped up as it had substantial foam on it. he did but stated it was full and the foam just went away. if it's filled up with foam, then it's not a full cup of coffee. wasn't the warmest either. place was clean and the staff were pleasant, just noticing a trend in bristol where you get half of your order and the rest is wastage

review: my main concern is that starbucks are still selling plastic bottles of water.  bottled water is unnecessary, there is tap water.  plastic production and pollution are major environmental problems.  why doesn't starbucks take a lead on this and dare to announce it will longer sell bottled water?  brilliant pr for starbucks and doing the right thing for the world!

author: JCPVP01
review: came in for a coffee and for my gf to use the bathroom. once my gf was in the loo and i was collecting the coffee, a female staff member went with keys (while there was a queue still of people that my gf stood in to get into the bathroom) and opened the door/ swung it wide open. a young guy, 2 younger females in the queue could see her with her pants down. oh and the till assistant was trying to let a friend in infront of the queue... no apologies to my gf just closed the door and started gossiping about it to her coworkers, to her surprise i told her off when she did this as i was waiting for my coffee. incident happened with girl who started her shift at 12:30 11/06/2024

author: Briana Lo
review:
on 22nd june (sat) 10:30-11:00, i went to collect my coffee. there’s a long queue of the till at that time.i spoke chinese so i overheard a conversation. 2 chinese girl has ordered their coffee and while they paid they already want to use the loo. they spoke to the staff while waiting the coffee and the staff just point them the direction of the loo, they went to check but it’s locked as you need keys. they wait until they pick up the coffee and asked again as the toilets are locked. then the staff asked them to queue again in the ordering queue to wait the staff to get the key, those girls are too shy and just don’t know what to do as the queue is so long and they already queue twice! once ordering once waiting for the drink, they should not queue the 3rd time!
i told the staff and the staff just roll their eyes and asked me to wait then they grab the toilet keys.
your staff really need to be trained, if they don’t like to work in a busy area, don’t do it. it’s really ashamed that you treat those costumer who can’t speak your language. that’s not okay!

review:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
starbucks coffee ☕🌍✨

a reliable stop wherever we travel — you always know what you’re getting, and that’s part of the charm! 💚

stopped in for a quick break and the coffee was perfectly brewed, smooth and just the right temperature. ☕👌 the baristas were friendly and quick, even with a long queue, and the place was clean, cozy, and comfortable.

grabbed a caramel macchiato and a slice of cake 🍰 — both delicious! the kids love the frappes too, so it’s an easy family win every time. 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦

whether for a morning caffeine fix, a laptop session, or a warm-up on a chilly day, starbucks always delivers. consistent, comforting, and welcoming. 🌟

review: located inside shopping centre, poor choice for gluten free, they used to do gluten free sandwiches but no longer do do. coffee ok if you don't mind the 5p charge for a cup. not the cleanest of starbucks. staff friendly , soya milk available just need to up their game and offer some gluten and dairy free food. updated in august , they have now stoped doing gluten free brownies and replaced them with wheat, avoid if you are gluten free. also no toilets here at starbucks. try coffee no.1 next door as they have facilities and good coffee.

author: Mike Davies

also, beware on sunday morning - sainsbury's will be shut, the nearest toilet is then in costa coffee. i wanted to give no stars - but couldn't work out how. mike

author: David Moore
review:
update october sun 29th 2023: 5! stars still .
yes the staff in here as on other days of late onwardly, other staff the same,  seem to be a very caring team . polite patient and caring way. thank you !
folk come back here i reckon for the key essence of coffee shops or the like: hospitality and hospitableness! a kind welcome and a nice seat with simple fayre of good. chocolate bars at £1.75 option and egg snacks give cheaper options for those of us less well of at the mo.
and!: simple welcoming kindness. and respect
5 or 5+ stars from me.

earlier review 8 months back :
an excellent no nonsense very pleasantly served starbucks.
lovely food options, & including simple chocolate bars, which along with other options are not quite such expensive options for those of us who don't want to or can't afford a large cake or sandwich etc option with a coffee.
young man at till serving strongly pleasant, and friendly to all customers style. very good. hope he, and they can have the patience to keep this up end of long days dealing with all we customers. perseverance in good things and a job, is a great key transferable skill.
i have onwards good experiences here.
it's own flavour style of coffee shops, for the mix to be good to know & which many of us appreciate and like. thanks ! .
every best for starbucks that they can do their business in the best of ways all round
thank! you .

author: John Tansey
review:
requested a decaf mocha and a tuna melt in a senior moment. i didn’t specify the size of drink and as if by default, was served the largest. is is unreasonable to have expected the staff member to have clarified the drink size? as an older person i tend towards smaller volumes. i like tuna because to me, it doesn’t smell particularly fishy, however, it did today and was not too my taste.
sadly not the best experience today but really enjoyed my mocha that i didn’t finish.

author: Christian Weale (Intrepid traveller)
review:
an exceptional team in the clifton down branch of starbucks.

do not be deterred by being placed at the entrance to a shopping centre.   there is seating both outdoors and on two levels inside.

what makes this branch exceptional is its team, they're consistently good,  a special shout out to rebecca and simon (manager).  they know their regular customers and approach customer service with a smile and in an informed manner.  it is clear that they have a sense of community and neighbourhood.

thank you to the entire team !

review: wow...very rude young-short white girl served me with a poker face/no smile, no eye contact, sternly asked "sittin in or owt!?". then failed to clean table i asked to be cleaned, begrudgingly she did the 3rd time but i had to ask again.  why is there no manager in tonight?  then 20 mins later, left front door open behind me, after bringing in outdoor furniture by 6pm.  this girl needs a serious check on her attitude & learn some basic customer care skills.

when she was making my drink she temporarily during that i had asked for a decaff coffee, as soon as she realised she made everything fresh again. she was chatty throughout and really seems passionate about her job.

i'm just sorry i didn't get her name so she can get the praise she so deserves.

review:
3 obnoxious servers, 2 young ladies with brown hair one curly and a young man served me today, the young lady with the curly hair rolled her eyes at me as i’d come in, no greeting, didn’t even ask me what i wanted to order. i’d ordered, didn’t get asked if i have a starbucks card, didn't get one thank you or any manors. when it was ready the young man hailed me down and said ‘here you go’. again, no thanks whatsoever, they were more interested in having their own conversation. i work in hospitality and understand conversations will be had, but when i’m waiting and watching them have a conversation after i’d ordered and not make my order, it really doesn’t make for a great experience. definitely won’t be going here again and it was my first time in this branch. very rude and i left very disappointed.

also, the cherry on top was my ice coffee was still hot🙃

author: El Ro
review:
friendly staff and good drinks, but hygiene is poor. dirty floor, tabes and chairs. toilets are dirty and not suitable for baby changing. for one of the priciest coffees in the area and city centre this is just disappointing. management needs to address this soon.
i don’t think i’m returning unless is the only option as these conditions have been the same for years.

author: JBull117
review: awful service - didn't even call out our order. both staff looked miserable.

author: pete murphy
review:
i always go in this store everyday, l have never had no problems at this store.
all the staff are really polite and helpful, both fargate and tudor square, i go in they always greet me, with a "hello" and a smile.
in fact i walk in and they do my order after saying hello to me. if there was a 20 out of 10 i would gladly give it.
well done both store, you do starbucks proud.....
pete.

author: Daz Burrows
review: popped in for a takeout coffee. we had a gift card which the girl initially couldn't work out, but her colleague managed to help her get sorted in the end.

review: not the best starbucks i have been in.  seats not very comfortable at all .

author: Christopher Perry
review: this starbucks has been recently renovated and is very modern. lots of seating available, and the staff are very friendly.

author: S. Shutt
review: having had a recent makeover this branch feels so much fresher and is more comfortable. i really appreciated the new easy chairs with arms which are much easier to get up from. i have arthritis and struggle with some seats, i was thrilled to find a cosy seat which i could actually get out of!  there was a good selection of pastries and cakes available, better than my previous visit when they only had cookies. the toilets have been refurbished. all in all a very good starbucks and just across from the museum. only downside is that many tables were taken up by long term laptop users leaving few seats available, got the last free table at 3.30 wednesday. it's a problem in most coffee shops these days, i don't know how it can be financially viable for a business to have so many tables taken up by folks buying one drink and then settling in for 2 or 3 hours!

author: Barrie Holland

review:

the drinks were ordered on the mobile app and we weren't told when they were ready, my friend had to go and ask for them and they had been sat on the collection bench for a while, as we had all ordered cold drinks it didn't affect the quality of them too much.

this is a lovely space to visit and sit and have a chat with friends.

review: there was a burly, tall female server with a very bad attitude. i just wanted a cup of coffee that wasn't hot, but i didn't want it iced either. she said no, and it was the only starbucks i've ever been to that refused such a request. she spoke very harshly. i initially thought she might just be someone who doesn't smile much, but she spent the next hour loudly chatting and laughing. i won't be going back to that place again.

author: suminge yes
review: whilst it can be a little noisy at times, this is to be expected of a coffee shop and understood. the staff are young, but so lovely and nice; with my server 'daisy' (who also had her name written in another language!) was so lovely, helped me decide what drink to order whilst keeping a smile on her face the entire time. when at busiest, a clear effort was made to keep the cafe clean whilst also cater to customers and their needs if necessary. i really recommend this starbucks, as it is also across from the park giving you lovely views for enjoying a quick cuppa. if the barista daisy who served me is there, you're sure to have a good time. :)

author: Ruby J Reid
review: staff should spend more time getting orders correct instead of calling other people drink orders disgusting while i was looking straight at them. due to my autism i need to add sugar free syrup to my drinks, i usually make my own drinks from home to avoid judgement but my espresso machine stopped working today and i decided to use the points i’d earned from when i used to work for starbucks to get myself a drink before uni. should’ve just deleted the app and left points unspent because now i’m going to have even worse anxiety when it comes to ordering now

author: aidan hazell
review: staff must not have much to do since they spend more time yapping about how people have their drinks instead of making them. terrible service, god forbid you have any alternatives in your drink. wish i could put 0 stars

review: clean, not busy. staff very polite

author: Rob Duffy
review: great chain of starbucks, well made drinks... can get a little busy!

review: terrible acoustics. the batista was clearing our table when i was away for a min. but clearly had jackets and bag left on chair.

author: Cheryl Wright
review: giving one star is too much. the service was awful! we ordered our coffee to sit in and went upstairs as there were no seats downstairs. upstairs was also quite busy, we sat with our coffees after we cleaned the chairs and table which were dirty. literally 3 minutes after we sat down an unpleasant young girl came up to say upstairs was shut!  she came to us directly and said we needed to move as they were shutting upstairs, we asked politely if there were any seats downstairs explaining it was already full when we came in and lots of people had been sent down, she replied "i don't know you'll have to go and look but upstairs is shut" i explained we'd only just bought our coffee to sit and drink to which she replied "it's not my problem" she was very rude and very obnoxious.  we went downstairs and asked for a refund as we'd not been able to sit and drink our coffees, we were given this but again, with an attitude! some serious staff training is needed, manners cost nothing. it would have been better had someone come up and said "we will be closing upstairs in 15 minutes, i'm sorry for any inconvenience" not "we are shut,  it's not my problem"

review:
poorly maintained and unpleasant

i recently visited this starbucks branch and found it to be a disappointing experience.
•	the store was very dark inside, which made it feel unwelcoming and hard to navigate.
•	there was no clear organisation—tables were cluttered and queues weren’t managed effectively.
•	most concerning were the toilets, which were disgustingly dirty and clearly not prioritised for cleaning.

given these issues, i would not recommend using this branch. starbucks typically sets a higher standard, but this location falls far short.

disgusted about my visit yesterday.  myself 5 year old daughter and mother in law sat outside. all other tables were occupied..the one we chose had ceramic dirty cups as well as left takeaway cups. we had to find another chair as the chairs had bird excrementa on too!! i went in and spoke to a staff member and asked politely if someone could come clean the table and clean the empty cups  away. without realising it was me the staff members spoke to another colleague...her name was ellie. she said that a lady has asked if the table can be cleaned and ellie looked with a screwed up face and said " she'll have to wait or she can clean it herself!!  she pointed at the cleaning stuff. i'm sorry what!!! i've just paid good money to have two drinks....and that's the remark i get!!! absolutely disgusting! birds excrements all over the tables because of the left over drinks. we took our drinks and walked away ....we wanted to sit down with my 5 year old so she could rest but it was disgusting! i wont be going back there again!!! i'll stick to costa! there were 4 members of staff in side, soone could have came out and quickly tidied up and cleaned the tables down! would of took less than 10 minutes! this ellie was rude and i watched how she was with other staff and customers too!!!

review: i've never been to a starbucks quite like this one! intervention is required! on walking in it was so dark and it smelt really odd, like rubbish bins or something. i ordered an iced chai tea latte to be told they haven't got that, so i said i'd have the normal chai instead- server then asked if i wanted it iced or hot ... couldn't help but laugh! there was a major lack of food, the cake shelves were just full of crumbs. we nipped to the toilet before leaving and it was clear they hadn't been cleaned for some time, it smelt so bad of urine and the floors were wet. honestly it's so gross considering this is an eatery too, staff didn't seem to want to be there either! starbucks are usually very nice venues to be in and take pride, however i'm surprised this one is still going. i'm guessing it won't be for long!

edit after a year:

author: Htet Kyaw
review:
the coffee served at this branch is satisfying and meets expectations in terms of flavor and quality. additionally, it offers free wi-fi access, which is very convenient, with an easy-to-navigate login process that enhances the customer experience.

the availability of "power sockets" is a plus, particularly for those looking to work or charge their devices. however, not all tables have access to these sockets, which can be inconvenient. expanding access to power sockets would greatly improve the usability of the space for customers.

one area for improvement is cleanliness. the tables are not cleaned promptly after customers leave, which detracts from the overall atmosphere. it would be better if staff ensured tables were tidied immediately. during my visit, the tissue supply was empty, which may or may not be a consistent issue, but it's something to monitor. service-wise, the waiting time for orders is noticeably, which can be frustrating. improving service speed would help align this branch with the higher standards often found in other regions.

similarly, the toilet cleanliness is subpar—acceptable but not ideal. finally, the business hours could use some adjustment. extending hours would make the café more accessible for late-night or early-morning customers.

overall, while starbucks maintains its reputation for decent coffee and useful amenities, there are several areas for improvement that would elevate the experience significantly.

review: massive thank you to tim. i had ordered via the app at 10:18; upon turning up after around 5 minutes it wasn't ready yet, understandable at how busy it was. however, after 10 minutes i asked if it had been made or not yet. tim was as welcoming as i could ask for, explained that it must have been taken and sorry for the wait. immediately he apologised and asked what was the order and kindly upgraded to a large drink for the issue. during my visit, not only was tim able to coordinate his colleagues to handle the queue, continued to make orders but also made the effort to make conversation with the elderly customers and making them feel welcome in store, building a personal relationship with them and noticeably remembered his repeat customers. outstanding from tim, asset to the store and starbucks. hopefully he gets recognised for his efforts!

author: Vincent G.
review: went out with my 10 month old this afternoon  and we went to this starbucks in newcastle and ordered coffee for my self and get everything settle for my baby’s lunch time when i accidentally tipped off my coffee off the table spilled everything on the floor. it was very struggling for especially i was alone with my baby. i went up to the counter while carrying baby to barrow a mop. “laura” one of the staff in starbucks offered her time on the counter to help me out despite during on busy hours. i was willing to clean everything but she insisted to assist me and offered me a new cup of coffee just to replace the one that i spilled. “laura” is very accommodating and kind. i hope the management would recognise her effort, attitude and selfless their staff displays. good service and i am pretty sure i’ll be back for another cup of coffee at this branch.

author: Alastair Semmence
review:
moved to the area recently and have been in 5-6 times. wouldn't usually post reviews online, but the services is so poor felt obligated.

barista's are super slow. even when in the queue with 1-2 people in front it takes up to ten mins to prepare 2-3 drinks that is just not good enough.

i used to work in hospitality and would have had a written warning if i performed so bad. expectation for 10mins on a machine would be 10-12 coffees, so 1-2 over 10 mins is so poor.

poor communication between staff from till to machine as well, the barista often not knowing what drink to make next 9 out of 10 times and having to ask customers what they ordered when the till operator has asked for your name and put a sticker on a cup, not a good system.

positives are that i have never had a bad cup of coffee, but the customer services just makes it a poor experience

author: Ryan Renaissance
review:
3.5/5.
probably my favourite starbucks in newcastle. i really like the location, and the outdoor seating - met a couple of interesting characters here that couldn’t have been more different from each other, a rather funny, bubbly student, and a charming older gent that was a poet. nice surroundings, and good for people-watching. i have many issues with starbucks but it does have the best chai latte out of the chains, probably best food too. basically, the coffee shop is good at everything but coffee, especially avoiding taxes but i digress… i find a lot of the baristas here really unfriendly and unwelcoming, even for starbucks’ standards which is a shame because even though i don’t go often, if i am going to starbucks, it’ll likely be this one. pretty mixed overall but a starbucks review was always going to be.

author: Alexander Hylton Parker
review:
i love this place. coffee and chat with a vibrant atmosphere of students and middle class professionals with laptops, important phone calls and a latte. there is plenty of seating, much of it by a window. the coffee and service are excellent, but it is the experience of the place that makes it fun.    the customers are wonderfully entertaining. sit with a coffee and listen to the conversations of budding egos with audible voices. hear them express their faith in the future and their value to the human race. watch as they politely compete to find their confidence in a hierarchy they hope to join one day.
it is close to jesmond dene and the city centre, in a small shopping area of restaurants, cafés and an oxfam bookshop. parking is easier on a sunday.

in my second visit, i decided to give the coffee shop another chance. however, the other barista's behaviour was still the same. she threw the straws on the wet table and made my second drink after attending to several other customers. i ordered two same kind of drinks, but when i reminded her of the second drink as i was waiting for so long, she responded rudely, insisting that she already knew.

i don’t know why the table always wet and don’t know if they don’t want to help why not put the straws out.

review:
i recently visited the starbucks located at jubilee place and i must express my concern regarding the lack of adherence to basic hygiene protocols by one of the staff members. while inside the establishment, i noticed one of the staff members coughing persistently without wearing a facial mask, which is concerning, especially given the current health climate. additionally, i observed that this staff member did not sanitize their hands after coughing, which is crucial for maintaining a safe and sanitary environment, especially in a food and beverage establishment.

as a customer, i value the importance of cleanliness and proper hygiene practices, particularly during these times. i hope that starbucks management takes this feedback seriously and ensures that all staff members are trained and consistently follow proper hygiene protocols to ensure the safety and well-being of both customers and employees.

i appreciate your attention to this matter.

author: Mark Stringer
review:
i was a regular at this coffee shop before the refurbishment.

i've got two problems with the new layout and decoration.

1. noise. the new interior has tiles on the floor and mostly hardwood furniture. the noise even when there are only a few customers in the cafe is extremely distracting. the curved window (of which more in a minute) always resulted in some weird acoustics but now that there are no wooden floors the noise levels are very high and distracting.

2. light. one of the great aspects of the old decor in this starbucks was that there were desk-style tables next to the curved shop-front window. these were under the skylight, so allowed customers to sit and use their computers in diffuse natural light.

suggestions for improvement:

* hang noise reducing things from the ceiling. put tapestries on the walls (a big ask, but it would make the space a lot more inhabitable).

* move the furniture around so at least some of the desks are up against the shop front window in the natural light.

author: emily peyton
review:
i want to make clear this low rating is not reflective at all of the coffee or the service or staff at this coffee shop who couldn't be better (starbucks food has never been the best but coffee, and in this store the staff, are brilliant)

no, the low rating is based purely on the moronically loud music now played in this starbucks which makes it impossible to work here (which many people do) and even talk sometimes. why coffee shops have to play music with heavy base is beyond me; it isn't a bar, it isn't a club. this base boost was obviously added when they did the recent refurb.

and on that note, the recent refurbishment (which no doubt cost an arm and a leg) made this erstwhile very pleasant coffee shop feel smaller, more garishly bright, and offers far less flexible seating options.

i used to really love coming here but today was my last time. i'm now relegated to using the tiny starbucks in the shopping centre, where the music is at a sane volume.

why, when they change things, do they so often ruin them?

author: SG 101
review:
i was having a good day until i went to this starbucks location and was disappointed by the service i received. i've been a regular customer here for years. the three staff members (black hijabi who was serving us, 2 asian girls on the side) at 14:40 on 27th of november 2024 at the tills, unfortunately, were quite rude during my order.
they seemed disinterested and even rolled their eyes while i was placing my order.
additionally, they didn't ask whether i wanted my order for takeaway or to eat in. when i clarified that i'd be eating in, they simply handed me a plate without any napkins. i've been to many starbucks and they normally always put the tissue on the bottom of the plate and then the dessert.
this lack of basic courtesy and attention to detail detracted from my experience. i hope this feedback encourages the staff to be more mindful of customer service in the future.

review:
stopped by with my missy and we had a great time at this starbucks. the cold drinks hit the spot — i had something like a mango dragonfruit refresher with ice, and she went for an iced caramel latte. perfect combo!

shoutout to the amazing staff — aylin, amanda, and feuzi — who made the experience even better. super friendly, we got along well, and they even made me laugh a few times. only thing missing? a smoothie! no matter how hard i tried, i couldn’t get one — maybe starbucks can add that to their to-do list! 😄

fun fact: the famous mermaid logo is inspired by mythical sirens who lure sailors, just like starbucks aims to attract customers with their drinks. the name “starbucks” comes from a character in moby-dick, adding a cool nautical touch.

i phoned starbucks customer service and they told me that basically they can charge whatever they want as it's operated by another company😡
i think starbucks is really disgraceful as there wasn't any price on show so you won't realise you have been charge   much higher for the same rolls you buy every week !!

review:
they got my order wrong and then were rude about it.

i ordered a frappucino with oat milk and vegan whipped cream, and then when i got my drink, it said "whole milk" on the label. i politely told the barista that there had been a mistake and he said "no i double checked with the person who took your order and he said you asked for regular milk".

i was just standing there waiting for my drink so he could have asked me directly, because why would anyone ask for vegan whipped but get cow's milk??

so i told him that i specifically asked for oat milk, and he just took the drink off my hand and remade it without a word.
i am appalled by this person's attitude, especially towards nice customers. i was not making a scene or being rude, but milk is an allergen it could have been a dangerous situation.

author: I G
review:
i am absolutely appalled by the service i received from angelina, the store manager. my experience was not just disappointing—it was infuriating. i was spoken to with a level of rudeness and disregard that is completely unacceptable in any customer service setting. i simply asked for a plastic cup with ice so i could share water with my daughter, after purchasing a double espresso and a large bottle of still water. angelina flatly refused my reasonable request, showing zero flexibility or understanding.

when i pressed for a reason, another staff member abruptly intervened, parroting a vague "head office policy" about recycling. angelina then claimed the cups had to be sent back to head office, which is utterly nonsensical. what makes this even more outrageous is that, on other occasions, i’ve been served drinks in plastic or paper cups without any issue. this inconsistency makes it clear that angelina was simply hiding behind excuses or making up her own rules.
the way i was treated made me feel completely unwelcome and disrespected as a customer. there was no attempt at empathy or proper explanation—just dismissiveness and a clear lack of professionalism. i left the store feeling angry and insulted.

i will never return to this location and strongly urge others to avoid it as well. staff, especially managers like angelina, need serious training in how to treat customers with basic courtesy and honesty. this was one of the worst customer service experiences i have ever had.

absolutely unacceptable.

author: Yoyo Cheung
review:
i had a disappointing experience at this starbucks. i initially ordered a tall matcha frappuccino but quickly changed my mind and asked to upgrade it to a grande size before the order was even processed. despite clarifying this right away, i was later given an tall iced matcha green tea latte instead — not what i ordered.

it was clear that the staff member taking my order (her name is angela if i remember) was unfamiliar with the menu or not properly trained. mistakes happen, but this one could have easily been avoided with a bit more attention. i hope the team receives better training going forward, especially in distinguishing between popular items on the menu.

would recommend double-checking your order if you visit.

author: Ian Needs
review:
stopped into starbucks kingston today and witnessed elida doing a valiant job. she was the only member of staff on duty – making drinks, serving customers, and trying to keep the place running single-handedly.

the café was packed with loud, sprawling groups of cyclists taking up space, queues of people waiting, and understandably a long wait for drinks. none of this was elida’s fault – she worked flat out and kept her composure impressively.

but it begs the question: why was she the only one working? it felt unfair on her and left customers frustrated. a busy starbucks in the middle of kingston shouldn’t be run by just one member of staff. poor management decision that let both staff and customers down.

author: What you need to know!
review:
overall: ★★☆☆☆

not the exactly the flagship starbucks. this cold, dirty and unclean starbucks is however conveniently located directly opposite wimbledon station. it’s very transient in feel which isn’t very nice and the staff not particularly into their jobs. not some where you’d consider a destination of any sorts to meet someone for a coffee and catch up.

starbucks is often reviled about their coffee but it’s actually very good to be fair especially the blonde roast. what is terrible is their food. their plastic wrapped croissants are a disgrace. (yes, they arrive in plastic and are unwrapped for show)

food: coffee with run of the mill substandard ‘pastries’

what i liked:

• coffee (blonde roast americano)
• location directly opposite wimbledon station

what i didn’t like:

• untidy, bordering dirty set up
• busy, cold atmosphere
• understaffed
• indifferent service
• heavy on the smokers lingering outside
• filthy tables

do i recommend going? not really
will i go again? probably
was it good value? yes

today i have a guy who just came off the street, smells really badly, has a plastic bag on his head, weed cigarette in his hand and is off to the toilet to smoke, is rubbing cream on his body while sitting with his belongings all around and just not great for your brand or your customers or for your business income.

last time i had a guys who covered his whole table, chair and floor in newspaper and sat eating a raw onion, a can of tuna and spread his belonging out and ate food from his packets... he never ordered one starbucks product either.

its tricky, but the upstairs room needs to be for customers who support your brand.

i don't think i will be back here.

review:

the restaurant is located in a quiet area and offers a beautiful view.

they have a wide variety of tasty products - of which i have tasted. the coffee is ok, and the teas are from the bag.

the staff is kind and nice.

the place seems to be well maintained, but the tables are not really clean.

one thing puzzled me: why does the air conditioner blow cold air when it's winter outside and it's already cold.

author: C. Marcus
review:
i bought a medium americano and water at c. 8:15 on 25/6/2024.

the male member of staff (paolo i think his name is) who took my order and name was very professional and pleasant.

the female member of staff who made the coffee was very rude. she dumped a cup of coffee down on the counter. others were waiting before me so how was i to know this was my coffee. when no one went for the coffee, she deigned to say it was an americano. no smile, no customer service. why take my name if you can’t be bothered to even say my name? did she profile me and decide i wasn’t worthy of decent customer service? why are these types of people with no customer care skills employed? is starbucks this desperate?

when i worked in the service industry as a student, we were taught better than this.

again, all other staff members have consistently been very friendly. initially, i thought the previous incident might have been due to a misunderstanding or a communication issue, but after today's experience, i'm unsure what the problem was.

i usually visit this starbucks all the time, but my recent two visits have been disappointing. although the cashier was friendly, the baristas on both occasions were impolite. when i ordered two takeaway coffee, and requested a carrier, they simply pointed to the coffee tray and acted as if i should have known where they are. although i found their behavior quite rude, i still thanked them. later, when i asked for a straw, one of the baristas threw it on the table, which was already wet, making the whole thing seem unhygienic. consequently, i decided not to take the straw.

in my second visit, i decided to give the coffee shop another chance. however, the other barista's behaviour was still the same. she threw the straws on the wet table and made my second drink after attending to several other customers. i ordered two same kind of drinks, but when i reminded her of the second drink as i was waiting for so long, she responded rudely, insisting that she already knew.

i don’t know why the table always wet and don’t know if they don’t want to help why not put the straws out.

review:
i recently visited the starbucks located at jubilee place and i must express my concern regarding the lack of adherence to basic hygiene protocols by one of the staff members. while inside the establishment, i noticed one of the staff members coughing persistently without wearing a facial mask, which is concerning, especially given the current health climate. additionally, i observed that this staff member did not sanitize their hands after coughing, which is crucial for maintaining a safe and sanitary environment, especially in a food and beverage establishment.

as a customer, i value the importance of cleanliness and proper hygiene practices, particularly during these times. i hope that starbucks management takes this feedback seriously and ensures that all staff members are trained and consistently follow proper hygiene protocols to ensure the safety and well-being of both customers and employees.

i appreciate your attention to this matter.

author: Mark Stringer
review:
i was a regular at this coffee shop before the refurbishment.

i've got two problems with the new layout and decoration.

1. noise. the new interior has tiles on the floor and mostly hardwood furniture. the noise even when there are only a few customers in the cafe is extremely distracting. the curved window (of which more in a minute) always resulted in some weird acoustics but now that there are no wooden floors the noise levels are very high and distracting.

2. light. one of the great aspects of the old decor in this starbucks was that there were desk-style tables next to the curved shop-front window. these were under the skylight, so allowed customers to sit and use their computers in diffuse natural light.

suggestions for improvement:

* hang noise reducing things from the ceiling. put tapestries on the walls (a big ask, but it would make the space a lot more inhabitable).

* move the furniture around so at least some of the desks are up against the shop front window in the natural light.

author: emily peyton
review:
i want to make clear this low rating is not reflective at all of the coffee or the service or staff at this coffee shop who couldn't be better (starbucks food has never been the best but coffee, and in this store the staff, are brilliant)

no, the low rating is based purely on the moronically loud music now played in this starbucks which makes it impossible to work here (which many people do) and even talk sometimes. why coffee shops have to play music with heavy base is beyond me; it isn't a bar, it isn't a club. this base boost was obviously added when they did the recent refurb.

and on that note, the recent refurbishment (which no doubt cost an arm and a leg) made this erstwhile very pleasant coffee shop feel smaller, more garishly bright, and offers far less flexible seating options.

i used to really love coming here but today was my last time. i'm now relegated to using the tiny starbucks in the shopping centre, where the music is at a sane volume.

why, when they change things, do they so often ruin them?

author: Tunde Kusika Taylor
review:
so this was probably the worst service i have received whilst at a starbucks; today is sunday the 7th july 2019 and i arrived at the starbucks around 5:30pm; there where two members of staff on duty a man and a woman and when i placed my order the gentleman at the counter was very blank and nonchalant towards the group of customers in-front of me but was also very unwelcoming when i placed my order.

the tables at the time where all filled with food items and i cleared my own table, when the gentleman did come to clear it up he seemed like it was something disgusting for him to be touching other peoples cups and plates and did not bother to spray and wipe down the table; the items that where out before he cleared it up seemed to have been there for a while.

the lady behind the counter who came out to stock the fridge was then very blunt and abrupt to a family that came in and that wanted to get some toasted items and continued to show attitude all the way to the till.
this same lady then just shouted out the cafe is closed and everybody has to leave with no notice, and when people where not moving fast enough she shouted again "its time to leave, we are closing," now we all know that a normal store will tell you 15 minute or how ever many minutes before so you can prepare yourself.

i certainly will not be going back to this particular starbucks; the two members of staff need training on customer service.

this starbucks is a great option for a coffee break just steps away from the famous liberty store. its central location makes it very convenient for those who want to relax after a shopping session or simply take a break during a walk in the area.

the coffee is good and carefully prepared, perfect for those who love classic drinks or want to try their always creative seasonal proposals. the staff is friendly and the atmosphere is welcoming, with comfortable seating both for those who want to stay a while and for those who prefer a “to go” option.

author: Nicole Robertson
review: i came in a couple of weeks ago. i ordered an iced vanilla latte with coconut milk. first the man at the counter was sighing and rolling his eyes, talking to his coworker and barely listening to my order. however he charged me the typical price i pay for this so i went to wait for my coffee as i was walking away they asked my name which i repeated three times (its nicole so not that difficult) and then as i’m waiting many people have come and gone with their coffees there is a hot vanilla latte with the name “ro” on it. so i asked if that was meant to be mine. a woman stepped in and asked if it was meant to be iced and with coconut milk i said yes then she lifted up a coffee that seemed to be half finished and said she thought that was mine. so i waited. then i watched them pour regular dairy into the cup which i cannot drink. so i said is that coconut milk? they binned it and started gossiping to eachother while making me a new one. so at this point i’ve been waiting about 8 minutes and being treated like absolute trash for no reason. i have been a barista before this is not a hard order. so she finally puts my coffee down and turns to another customer who has also been waiting and says i might have grabbed his coffee instead of mine meanwhile that coffee that was there in the first place was there before he even walked in the door. i said to the lady this was not my fault and that i made a simple order with three different baristas. as i’m walking out of the shop i take a sip and realise there is no vanilla. i just threw it in the bin. what a waste of my time and money. you guys really need some education on basic customer service and basic listening skills. i am english it is not like i had a difficult accent to understand or wasn’t english speaking at all. at any of my jobs in hospitality they wouldnt stand for this type of behaviour. absolutely ridiculous

author: Grace Harper
review:
this is by far the worst experience i have had in this particular starbucks.
the staff were talking (or maybe i should say screaming) at eachother the entire time which lead to delays and messing up orders, i gave them my own cup and they literally set it aside and forgot then proceeded to give me my drink in a paper cup (obviously trying to avoid using a paper cup by bringing my own...), when they decanted it into my cup the drink was obviously very different and i couldn't taste the syrup in my caramel macchiato, they proceeded to tell me the drink comes with two pumps, i said i know but i can't taste it at all so asked if they could add an extra pump (they added three... i didn't ask to be given diabetes thank you very much). they then left my partner's sandwich on the side and we had to tell them that it is ours and we want it warmed up, then they essentially forgot to make his drink.
less chatting and focus so silly mistakes like this don't happen, usually they are very efficient and the drinks are made to perfection but this was very disappointing, i shall be going somewhere else until the staff have realised that standing there laughing and chatting is not what customer service is about, please sort this out. get the orders right then you can scream and laugh with eachother.

review: came here got served at the till by a lovely girl then asked for a coffee with no cream, the guy making the drinks clearly didn’t read the ticket which i wasn’t to bothered about but then my boyfriend order a drink with a shot of coffee because that’s what he wanted and payed extra just for the guy to make it without coffee then when we went to tell the guy we wanted coffee in it he rudely says “there’s no coffee in that drink”, well yes we know that, that’s why we payed extra for the shot. then he continues to make the drink exactly how it was done before. i get it can be frustrating to remake something, i work in hospitality and understand however because we payed for the shot that’s the only reason why we asked for a remake. so over all, tell the guy to not be cheeky and remake something exactly how it was made the first time i don’t think it’s that deep to  just add the coffee we payed for.

author: Euan Mossman
review: came in today, ordered with a lady at the till, delightful person. a caramel frappuccino with no cream on top and a cookies and cream frappuccino that doesn’t usually come with coffee but i had enquired about adding coffee which i was told i could pay to add, i then payed for espresso to be put into the drink. the gentlemen making the drinks clearly in a rush… (when my girlfriend and i are the only people ordering) was clearly not reading the tickets coming through. the caramel frappuccino came out with cream on top and the cookies and cream frappuccino had no coffee in it. i tried to query this and the gentlemen was rude and dismissive, turning away from me as i try to speak to him , telling me there’s not meant to be coffee in the drink and i explain i’ve paid for it to be added. this angers him and he snatched the drink back only to remake the drink exactly the same… i work in hospitality… i’m not an idiot, i watched him do it and he couldn’t give a toss. didn’t even try to make it look like he put espresso in it. absolute joke but pop off i guess.

author: SG 101
review:
i was having a good day until i went to this starbucks location and was disappointed by the service i received. i've been a regular customer here for years. the three staff members (black hijabi who was serving us, 2 asian girls on the side) at 14:40 on 27th of november 2024 at the tills, unfortunately, were quite rude during my order.
they seemed disinterested and even rolled their eyes while i was placing my order.
additionally, they didn't ask whether i wanted my order for takeaway or to eat in. when i clarified that i'd be eating in, they simply handed me a plate without any napkins. i've been to many starbucks and they normally always put the tissue on the bottom of the plate and then the dessert.
this lack of basic courtesy and attention to detail detracted from my experience. i hope this feedback encourages the staff to be more mindful of customer service in the future.

review:
stopped by with my missy and we had a great time at this starbucks. the cold drinks hit the spot — i had something like a mango dragonfruit refresher with ice, and she went for an iced caramel latte. perfect combo!

shoutout to the amazing staff — aylin, amanda, and feuzi — who made the experience even better. super friendly, we got along well, and they even made me laugh a few times. only thing missing? a smoothie! no matter how hard i tried, i couldn’t get one — maybe starbucks can add that to their to-do list! 😄

fun fact: the famous mermaid logo is inspired by mythical sirens who lure sailors, just like starbucks aims to attract customers with their drinks. the name “starbucks” comes from a character in moby-dick, adding a cool nautical touch.

author: Giampiero Rigari
review:
hi. i have never liked starbucks as its dark roasted coffee is very bitter and taste quite cheap as quality.
however this morning i really wanted a coffee and i went for a flat white asking for the lighter roasting speciality which they offer these days.
unfortunately the profile of that coffee disappears completely in the flat white, not sure because is too light or because it requires less milk or for other reasons.
i found really strange that a pan a chocolate at 6am taste stale and shriveled and the chocolate filling is crunchy.
when i go to greggs or wenzel s the pastries taste much better even if the coffe is not great, but at least you can taste the profile and you spend almost half price.
sorry guys but for me it's a no no.

absolute waste of money. how can you get a drink wrong three times?! absurd!

author: J Bateman
review:
quite possibly the dirtiest and most depressing starbucks in london. congratulations. this branch urgently needs a complete overhaul. the establishment's prime location opposite the ealing broadway tube station seems to have fostered complacency rather than excellence.

from grime-covered tables to the general atmosphere of neglect, it's evident that this starbucks has seen better days. the lack of cleanliness is unacceptable, especially in a city where coffee culture is thriving and there are numerous other options for a quality brew. the only thing this branch has going for it is its convenience for commuters, but even that shouldn't justify the subpar conditions.

the management appears to be non-existent or grossly ineffective. the solution is simple: close the branch temporarily, bring in a competent manager, and start from scratch. with so many lovely coffee shops in ealing, there's no excuse for this starbucks to be the worst in the area.

for now, i'd recommend avoiding this starbucks and exploring the myriad of other delightful coffee spots in ealing. save yourself the disappointment and seek out a place that values both its customers and its coffee.
