author: Dimitrii Toma
review:
never go to this store. awful experince. misleading and unfair parking rules – avoid this location!

be extremely careful if you’re parking at southgate park near starbucks (stansted). the signs are unclear and do not clearly explain that you must enter your vehicle registration inside the starbucks store to qualify for the “free 60 minutes.” there’s no mention of this at the entrance or near the parking bays.

even worse, the signage says “southgate park customers only” – but what does that mean? there’s no clear explanation that only starbucks customers are allowed to use the parking. if you’re not aware of the hidden requirement to register your car inside the shop, you’ll receive a £100 fine.

this is an unfair trap, and i believe it’s designed to confuse and penalize people.

i strongly advise others to avoid this car park unless you’re absolutely sure of the rules — and even then, take photos of the signs!

author: S F
review:
this is not a scam, just a normal car park which you adhere to like every other car park that you park in!!

this car park is quite simple if you take the time to read the actual facts!

it is clearly advertised as starbucks customer car park (not a free waiting zone to collect from the airport)
2 options, become a paying customer and register your vehicle in store, and receive 1 hour free parking or pay to park via the ringgo app.
if the store is closed then you must pay via the app.
follow these simple rules and you can walk anywhere!
mcdonald’s customers park in mcdonalds car park.
simple!

starbucks do not run or gain monetary value from this car park. the rules are set by the land owners, who use met to enforce these rules.

a lovely big car park for the lovely customers of starbucks!

once again i’d like to highlight this to those human beings that are unable to read the signs in place, all around the car park and in the shop.

very clear signage on lamp post and in windows of the store.

do not believe the factually incorrect information you read online!

now havjng dogs in a food establishment is gross but i respect policy i was simply asking that the dogs didn't walk around and upto the collection counter it was a very fluffy dog  and aside from my allergie it is unhygenic.
to  be  told to leave and not come back over a dog is disgusting i am friendly and loyal, there was no discussion, im guessing starbucks prefer dogs over loyal paying customers. i wont be going back to any starbucks and i shall be putting this on my social media platfroms, awful woman should be sacked. the other staff are lovely. shame on you starbucks dogs over paying customers indeed

author: Sagal Ahmed
review:
this is hands down the worst starbucks i’ve ever been to. i’m constantly amazed at how unfriendly and miserable the staff are—it's like customer service is a foreign concept here. most of the time, the coffee is either burnt or made with zero effort, and the team looks completely checked out. they’re disorganized, can’t handle a small rush, and never make drinks in order.

i’ve kept going back because i work nearby and unfortunately, it's the least bad option in the area—but i’ve finally had enough. the staff bring the whole vibe down with their attitude and sloppy work. honestly, this place doesn’t even deserve a one-star rating. the manager looks so unkempt i was shocked to find out he was the manager. this whole branch needs a serious reset.

author: B K
review:
whilst walking past a starbucks in westfield  @ shepherds i thought i treat myself to a toastie & coffee as last time i checked in was about 3 months ago.

at the till a member of staff advised me to top up my starbucks app on my phone instead of doing it at the till to get the reward points.

typical now - starbucks wi-fi not working, app crashes asking for an update, the barcode scanner was tried about 9 times (9 beeps) - hit and miss. staff open a second till as now 2 tolls are required to sort this out.

10 minutes after - job done. i head to find a table - i find one and it’s 1m apart from other people, whilst the tables opposite are face to face / back to back seating.

i’ve just started on the sandwich the moment staff approached me and said i am sitting at “please dont use” table and have to leave.

i though i’ll ask if there are any free tables - obviously answer was “no”. the coffee also wasn’t right - full house of issues.

haha, what a mistake to turn into this starbucks and waste that much time and spend around £10 for a coffee and sandwich to get that. needless to mention the new reward system wants you to spend £50 to get 1 free drink ! this must be going downhill.

thanks to some lovely young ladies that offered me a seat and decided to go.

this place is shambles so to speak and i think i won’t be making a mistake going to any starbucks again considering all the above.

author: a h
review:
starbucks at highbury recently underwent a refurbishment, and the new look is quite impressive. during my visit, the cafe wasn't very busy, despite having three staff members on duty. however, i was disappointed to find that the tables were unclean, which was surprising considering the presence of staff members.

i ordered a cappuccino through the mobile app, but unfortunately, it did not meet the usual starbucks standard. it was served extra hot, even though i did not request it to be prepared that way. this was a bit frustrating, as it affected the overall taste and enjoyment of my drink.

on the positive side, the refurbished interior of the starbucks at highbury is visually appealing. the modern decor and comfortable seating make it a pleasant place to relax and enjoy a cup of coffee. the ambiance was calm and inviting, allowing for a peaceful atmosphere to unwind or catch up with friends.

despite the disappointment with the cleanliness of the tables and the temperature of my cappuccino, the staff members were friendly and attentive. they were quick to take my order and assist with any questions or requests i had. their efficient service added a touch of professionalism to the overall experience.

in conclusion, the recently refurbished starbucks at highbury has a visually appealing interior, but it falls short in terms of cleanliness and attention to detail. the cappuccino i ordered was served at an extra hot temperature, which was not to my liking. however, the friendly staff members made up for some of the shortcomings. with a bit more focus on cleanliness and consistency in the preparation of drinks, this starbucks location could provide a more enjoyable experience for its customers.

author: Aaron Henriques
review:
in store the staff are good and most things are usually fine. however, for delivery orders, they're consistently poor.

although the frequent fault of uber eats lack of ability to get a rider to the location, it is in the control of the staff at starbucks to ensure that the "hot drinks" they allow to leave the premises when its collected 20-30 minutes after ordering, does at least leave hot.

i've yet again wasted over £12 on two drinks that were made 30 minutes before it was even collected by uber.

if the staff have seen the drinks sat there for 20+ minutes, logic to me would say that they do not allow it to go out like that and instead they make it again when the rider turns up. even better, just do not make the order until they turn up.

it happens so frequently, there is no feedback mechanism and uber eats wash their hands of it. if starbucks and uber were forced to provide refunds each time, their ability to resolve this issue would swiftly improve.

neither uber eats or starbucks corporation seem to care about product quality and standards, and that trickles down to the end delivery point.

rutvik, is a bit of a legend. i've been coming here for the last few months. always serves me with a big smile. knows my order. this creates a local small independent feel, even though starbucks is massive. the seating area is cosy downstairs. perfect for working, a date, interview or just catching up with a few mates.

my only feedback would be healthier food options and better wifi. if the food options were better i would spend more here. everything is highly processed.

review:
i have been a loyal customer and i love starbucks - i usually get my coffee from this branch as i just live above the shop next to it! however they don’t always have my favourite coffee which is filter coffee and i always been offered “americano” which is not same ! every time i have been offered i didn’t mind waiting for my filter coffee to get ready .

yesterday i was in an absolute rush and i ordered online ! 20 minutes later when i went down and took a sip of my coffee it was “americano “ i have convinced the staff that it was americano and they casually said someone else grabbed your coffee which i am not sure about! and then they gave me such an old and cold filter coffee that i have noticed when i just stepped out the shop! i have gone back to shop and asked for a proper filter coffee despite to my limited time!
however , i was late for my appointment- was annoyed about the experience!

i have ordered online it is because i didn’t want to be late ! please re-train your staff and teach them the difference between americano and filter coffee

author: Ricardo Roxo
review:
the starbucks at surrey quays shopping centre, located at 13-15 surrey quays, unit b, london se16 7ll, is a popular spot among locals and shoppers. due to its high foot traffic, finding available seating can be challenging, especially during peak hours. the store’s limited size contributes to this issue, suggesting that an expansion could enhance customer comfort.

while starbucks is renowned for its coffee quality, some patrons have expressed concerns about the food offerings at this location. specifically, the value for money regarding food items, such as the thinness of cake slices relative to their price, has been a point of critique. this indicates potential room for improvement in the food menu to meet customer expectations.

in summary, while the surrey quays starbucks maintains the brand’s standard for quality beverages, addressing issues related to seating capacity and food value could significantly enhance the overall customer experience.

author: John Russell Powell
review:
i’ve never been fond of starbucks. their tea and coffee are both not very enjoyable. but we were at canada water an hour early for an exhibition, and the kids were hungry. nothing else was open yet, so we popped in here. grabbed a croissant and cinnamon bun. the bun was cold, so asked if they could heat it up (especially since it’s supposed to be served hot (not to mention it wasn’t the first thing in the world either). was told they were not allowed to heat it up because it has the glaze on it. the excuse was so silly and absurd. i honestly couldn’t even process it for a minute. it would be like going to an italian restaurant and being served pasta, this cold, because they can’t heat it up since it has sauce on it.

thank you for reminding me why i should never where is my time or money at this business.

author: Klaudia Maria
review: appalling conduct and zero accountability at surrey quays starbucks. my mother and i were verbally harassed by a staff member over a private conversation, with the employee verbally assaulting us in our language so no one could grasp the full rudeness of her words. several customers witnessed the incident, yet the manager stood by and did nothing meaningful. this has now been formally reported to starbucks uk customer care, with further escalation to the equality advisory and support service, as the manager has failed to take any proper action.

author: Louise
review: came for a coffee at surrey quays starbucks, ended up watching a staff member shouting at customers in a language no one else could understand while the manager just stood there clueless - safe to say i won’t be back!

author: Marley J
review: speedy service and kind staff! the abuse the workers and manager have to deal with from problematic members of public is very upsetting. the manager is always nice to customers and has turned this branch from a sad lifeless place to a much nicer better environment that i keep coming back to. staff are better trained, coffee quality is much better and the atmosphere is truly great. the great manager always deals with conflict in a professional, customer centred way, and remains calm even when the customer isn’t in the right! kudos to him and the team 😀

review: i went into this starbucks at 8:05am on 19/11/21 and ordered a coffee frap with less ice (i emphasised on the less ice a few times). the male barista who took my orders was very polite and then passed my cup over to another male barista who began to make my drink. not only did he put even more ice than usual but then when i told him i had asked for less ice he didnt respond and then threw the made drink into the sink. he made my new drink and i asked him if i could have a flat clear lid instead of the round ones that you would need a straw one for. he then said no these are the lids we give for frapps (which isn’t true because i always have a flat lid with my cold cofffees) when i asked him if i could have one anyway he chucked it on the counter top and walked away. i’m so shocked by this service because i come into this starbucks nearly every morning and i’ve never had an issue like this. the other members of staff were super nice but this one barista really ruined my morning.

ridiculous branch.

not only her manners were very unkind but she lied to me too. and all of this for what? to save a scoop of coffee? is it made out of  gold? it wouldn't costed them 10 p and u was charged £5.5 for that coffee. she has no idea what service or manners is and this revolted me.

author: Lili
review:
i ordered a lavender latte (£6.10) via the app and went to the store to pick it up. the app showed that my order was ready, yet when i arrived, the staff were busy making hot drinks for other customers. i waited patiently for them to finish, but when i finally asked about my order, i was told that the lavender latte was unavailable. shockingly, i was informed that they could not process a refund and instead asked if i wanted to choose another drink.

i agreed and selected a matcha latte (large, £5.10). the staff initially assumed the two drinks were the same price, but i pointed out the £1 difference. again, i was told that their system could not refund the difference. this is completely unacceptable and absurd for any service-oriented business.

after waiting over 20 minutes in total, i finally received my matcha latte, which turned out to be mostly foam. i only realized this after i left the store. i had to return and ask if they could remake

this experience was frustrating, disappointing, and a complete waste of my time. i sincerely hope you will review your service processes and train your staff to handle such situations more professionally.

author: Ben lin
review: my husband and i was traveling london two weeks ago. on sept 22, around 4:40 pm, after visiting westminster abbey, and walking some hours around the thames, my husband and i walked into o2 starbuck. we got a coffee and sat by a table for a little rest. just a few minutes after we we sat there and enjoyed coffee, the young guy in the store came to us and said, “ we are closing the store in 10 minutes.” and he began to spray the tables near us for cleaning . obviously he told us to leave the store. although we felt strange, because starbuck stores in america never close so early, we still got up and walked out of the store. on their store glass wall, we saw their business time, it showed the store close at 17:00. but today when i check the o2 website and it shows this starbuck store operating time is actually from 8am to 9pm, i feel quite outraged. what they did to my husband and me definitely violates the business contract. they don’t have right to force us out of the store by telling a lie that they will close the store. i hope starbuck will punish them for their arrogance and disrespect to customers and their violation to the business rules.

author: Ahlam Al-Zahrani
review:
i had a very disappointing experience at this starbucks branch. my elderly mother urgently needed to use the restroom. we asked an asian-looking female staff member if there was a restroom available, and she said it’s only for customers. so i purchased a coffee just so my mother could use the bathroom.

after paying, she told me the restroom was dirty and out of service — something she clearly knew before i made the purchase. when i asked for a refund, she rudely told me the manager wasn’t available and that i would have to wait at least 10 minutes, with no guarantee.

she refused to give her name when i asked. her tone was very dismissive and unprofessional throughout the interaction. extremely poor customer service and a lack of basic empathy, especially considering my mother’s age and the situation. i don’t expect this kind of treatment from starbucks.

this was on 6/7/2025 at 18:50 pm uk time.

review:
absolutely unacceptable behavior. the main manager (black hair, eyeliner with green aipron —unfortunately didn’t catch her name) was openly out loud scolding staff in front of customers, which is incredibly unprofessional and embarrassing. she was also discussing private work matters and his position out loud in a way that should never happen on the floor.
as someone who has worked in hospitality, i can say this is a complete no-go. staff should be treated with respect, not spoken down to as if they’re lesser or incapable. i can only imagine how draining it must feel for employees to face this kind of treatment every day.

if you’re unhappy with your role or your life, then do something and make a change. don’t take it out on the people around you.

author: virgin media
review:
had a horrible time at this starbucks.

i came in the shop and asked for the washroom, they directed me to the disability bathroom as it was the only one available in the establishment. i finished my business in there and found that there was no toilet paper. not one single roll. i was left stranded.
i pull on the call assistance rope hoping to get one of the staff member's attention but after pulling it down it was ignored for 10 minutes straight until i realised that it was broken. a broken "call assistance" in a disability washroom is horrid to say the least.

i powered on my almost dead phone and called the place hoping to get a staff members attention. it rang for about a minute before someone answered. as i explained my situation the person on the other end sounded uninterested and replied with a simple "okay". finally i was brough the toilet paper.

afterwards, i spoke to manager on duty/supervisor, the one who answered the phone and told him what happened. he gave me an unapologetic "there's nothing we could do" which is a shame as i understand the assistance rope can't be repaired that moment but the restocking of toilet paper needs to have better attention on it.

felt totally embarrassed and helpless, it's going to be funny to look back on but felt extremely horrible in the moment. either way, 1/5 service. can't talk about food or atmosphere so 3/5.

review: went there on saturday. the guy who served us made a joke about how he was making mistakes all day( i am not going to name and shame) as he was new and it was real busy, hot, loud. we ordered a oat chai iced latte (making sure we said oat latte more than 1time). service was slow because 90% of the orders before ours were wrong(not just cos it was busy). when we finally got our order i took a sip and it just tested off and nasty, to me. not the normal nuttiness you'd get from oat milk. i checked the label and i was served semi skimmed milk. that literally made me puke and felt like crying.  this is a real and serious matter. i understand it is my choice being vg but what if that was severed to a person with an intolerance or allergy, what were the consequences going to be?! the guy serving the coffee did not apologise(it was really busy and i understand his frustration as orders before ours were also put through the till wrogly) and he justify himself saying they just did want the order said(fair enough). train your till ppl to listen to the customer. show them the consequences of not doing so and serving allergy items to your customers who might have no choice but drink alternative milks.

author: Val D
review: sasha had followed no health & safety codes while making drinks. touching her face and hair while making drinks and handling food, which was disappointing to see. she did not make the drinks correctly (not enough ice, not enough coffee, and the cold foam was on the bottom of the drink as if it had sunk instantly). she was also unpleasant and unfriendly. the man working the till, unsure of his name, was a little nicer, but seemed unhappy to be doing his job. the coffees also tasted strange, presumably from being made incorrectly. i would avoid this place if you can; the whole experience is a letdown for the starbucks company.

author: Nico Gray
review: i recently visited this starbucks on the way back from kew gardens and was thoroughly disappointed. the staff were very rude and inattentive, with the woman making the coffees virtually whispering what she'd made then shouting it again very loudly. the man who served us on the till looked at us for a while before actually serving us, and then my partner had to repeat their order several times as he kept repeating back the wrong item. i ordered a crème brulée brown sugar frappucino which had a very strange consistency, which i assume is due to using very little ice... not what i expect from a frappucino! we will not be returning.

review:
i went to starbucks this morning for coffee. i specifically mentioned while ordering that it was for have-in, but when the drinks were ready, they were given in takeaway cups. i politely told the staff member that i wanted them in mugs instead, as i had requested. instead of simply correcting the order, she became rude and started raising her voice, questioning why i needed mugs.

as a regular customer who comes to starbucks every day, i found this behavior very unprofessional and offensive. i had to explain that i had paid for my drinks and that it is my choice how i’d like to have them. despite that, she continued mumbling and showing a poor attitude.

this kind of treatment is disappointing, especially at a place we visit daily. starbucks should ensure staff treat customers with respect and provide service without unnecessary arguments.

review: ordered drinks and then 2mins later told "we are closing this section off". given that the space is limited and some of the available space had the stools already on the table this was a shabby thing to do. this was over an hour before the closing time. the place is run for the convenience of the staff rather than customers. fortunately there are plenty of alternative coffee shops nearby, so bye bye starbucks in kew.

author: Ina Busuioc
review: the coffee tasted amazing, and i got a chocolate muffin and that was delicious as well

author: Hana Gru
review: today, on the morning shift, two ladies serving are very unpleasant and one of them has the impression that she works there as a punishment. today, when she saw a large queue (4 people), she rolled her eyes. i think that customer service is her job and she is behaving very unpleasantly. this is not the first situation as i use this cafe often. the other people working there are very nice and friendly, but these two are a disaster. maybe a customer service course would be useful.

author: erik laers
review: this girl was so rude?? like what have some manners 😭😭?

author: Deljane Kum-Naksch
review: for me personally, starbucks cafes are mainly good for working. you’ve got free wifi & no one is bothered how long you stay &/or how much you order or spend. however, coffee wise, it’s poor quality & food is just ok.

author: Val D
review: sasha had followed no health & safety codes while making drinks. touching her face and hair while making drinks and handling food, which was disappointing to see. she did not make the drinks correctly (not enough ice, not enough coffee, and the cold foam was on the bottom of the drink as if it had sunk instantly). she was also unpleasant and unfriendly. the man working the till, unsure of his name, was a little nicer, but seemed unhappy to be doing his job. the coffees also tasted strange, presumably from being made incorrectly. i would avoid this place if you can; the whole experience is a letdown for the starbucks company.

author: Nico Gray
review: i recently visited this starbucks on the way back from kew gardens and was thoroughly disappointed. the staff were very rude and inattentive, with the woman making the coffees virtually whispering what she'd made then shouting it again very loudly. the man who served us on the till looked at us for a while before actually serving us, and then my partner had to repeat their order several times as he kept repeating back the wrong item. i ordered a crème brulée brown sugar frappucino which had a very strange consistency, which i assume is due to using very little ice... not what i expect from a frappucino! we will not be returning.

author: Mohamad Shahriari
review: rude staff. especially nina

author: Bashkim Maloku
review: my favourite starbucks! delicious coffees and an amazing staff! oana she is the best always smiling and happy to serve i strongly recommend!

author: Fren Blue
review:
i’ve been here this morning at 7.30; the service has been great. i think the name written on the uniform was ramon.
smiling face, (even if was so early), gentle in mood, (even if i can be a very pretending client).
i went out and he said goodbye, his colleague, a girl, she didn’t. i mean simple things he did but he made a better atmosphere.
everything was really clean.
he can definitely do his job.

author: ruhi sodhi
review:
i went to starbucks this morning for coffee. i specifically mentioned while ordering that it was for have-in, but when the drinks were ready, they were given in takeaway cups. i politely told the staff member that i wanted them in mugs instead, as i had requested. instead of simply correcting the order, she became rude and started raising her voice, questioning why i needed mugs.

as a regular customer who comes to starbucks every day, i found this behavior very unprofessional and offensive. i had to explain that i had paid for my drinks and that it is my choice how i’d like to have them. despite that, she continued mumbling and showing a poor attitude.

this kind of treatment is disappointing, especially at a place we visit daily. starbucks should ensure staff treat customers with respect and provide service without unnecessary arguments.

author: Alessandra F
review:
i stopped going to starbucks over a decade ago when i found out that they conduct their business in the uk but don't pay taxes in the country.
occasionally i am invited for coffee and i can't refuse, as it happened when i visited this branch in paddington station.
the interior is quite run down to be honest, the decor is dated and the tables and chairs were dirty and stained so i have had to clean up before i sat- as nowadays the staff calls out for you to collect the drinks nobody cleans tablets anymore and i don't want to drink my coffee sitting on a pile of crap- because the table was sticky like glue.
the place is poorly lit and looks very cluttered, i really didn't like it and i couldn't wait to get out.
the atmosphere was miserable, there was a woman with a huge dog sitting inside which is always a no no in a place serving food.
i prefer pret, 100% better service and drink selection.

author: まなよこ
review:
the female cashier is unfriendly. the guy making drinks is also unfriendly. when a drink is handed to you, it's handed to you by sliding it onto a table like a bowling ball, without asking anyone.
i politely handed it to the white man in line behind me.

i'm japanese, and i was surprised at how different the level of customer service was compared to starbucks in japan and korea.

author: Giang Le
review: i ordered an iced matcha latte to go. they gave me a hot chai latte. i said to the lady in nicely way that it was wrong since i ordered an iced matcha latte. they give me a new one which is the right thing that i have ordered but the taste was not the same (seems like watery drink). then, the counter guy came to me with the bill and said that “next time you should have to clarify it was iced matcha latte not chai latte”. it was awful experience and terrible customer service in here since it was not mine problem. i always ordered that drink in many different starbucks stores and every times i got the right one since they confirmed the order again for the customers

review: slowest, most lackadaisical service i have seen - even in london. staff all had their backs to the counter, ‘very’ slowly dabbing at a counter or plating a sandwich. three staff members but it took five minutes before someone even turned around to take my order. slow, shuffling steps and glazed, confused expressions all round.

review: ordered a double espresso and just requested hot water on the side. i even bought my own mug. i wanted hot water on the side as i like to add it slowly and to my taste. the staff member refused me and said we are not allowed. i am using my own mug so i don't understand what difference it makes? she said she can add the hot water to my espresso directly. i said no i want it seperate or if it's to much bother then i don't want you to be in trouble? she replied thank you so much i will get into trouble from my manager. i feel that it is such a shame that the manager of the store would not trust staff to do this and treat your employees so poorly that they can't even fulfil a small request. i hope the area manager reads this and reprimands the manager to loosen up a bit with the team.

amazing job starbucks for helping us when we were stranded… also, the girls working this location this afternoon around 4 pm were absolutely amazing!

author: QiHan Li
review:
this was an unbelievably bad experience — honestly, i left the store feeling shaken and kind of scared.

i walked in just wanting an iced americano. that’s it. instead, i was greeted by an asian female staff member with one of the worst attitudes i’ve ever seen. when i said my order, she gave me this incredibly annoyed look, stared at me like i was stupid, and snapped, “what do you want?” like i was bothering her just by speaking. i repeated my order, and she mumbled something i couldn’t quite catch. when i politely asked her to repeat it, she replied with this nasty, sarcastic tone that honestly made me feel like i had committed some kind of crime just by asking for coffee.

i didn’t ask for anything weird. i just wanted a basic drink. normally, i’d check her name tag so i could make a proper complaint — but i was genuinely afraid to even look at her again. i kid you not, i felt like if i stared too long, she might actually hit me. that’s how hostile her energy was.

i have no idea why she treated me that way — like i had personally offended her just by existing.
and if she didn’t want to serve me, she could’ve just said so — i wouldn’t have cared.
what really upset me was the way she chose to treat me: with sarcasm, hostility, and honestly, a vibe that felt borderline discriminatory.

i walked out feeling humiliated and low-key terrified. i’m never coming back. i don’t need to be looked up and down, talked down to, and made to feel like trash when all i wanted was a damn iced coffee.

hard pass. never again.

review: if there was a competition for the worst starbucks in london – this starbucks would have won it immediately! asian lady who works here is absolutely unprofessional, impolite and unfriendly, i wanted to walk away right after ordering my drink and seeing her attitude towards the costumers. cherry on top – they can’t properly prepare a good coffee, it tastes so bad here! their manager – rude black woman is yelling at her team in front of all the visitors. awful experience 😔 i will avoid this toxic place from now on.

author: Sandra B
review:
roughly 5/6 months ago, my husband i changed from our usual sainsbury’s to nine elms because we could have a starbucks date after we do a food shop.
in the beginning i really enjoyed it, the only fault at that time was no one was clearing tables or emptying the bin enough.
after not being able to go for a while, we started going again roughly a month ago and i have noticed the staff’s attitude. they clearly do not enjoy any part of their jobs and it reflects on their service, and the ones that are just talk and gossip, usually leaving one employee begrudgingly doing everything else.
from my experience, if staff aren’t happy, there’s something wrong with the company’s system. clearly hq needs to step in and help incentivise staff a lot better than what they’re doing now because it’s obvious from these reviews that it’s reflecting on the service and cleanliness of the location.

author: Viviana Olivetto
review:
i visited on friday 27th september 2024, between 3pm and 5pm. i have a health condition which requires me to have regular access to the bathroom. when trying to use the bathroom, these were locked and i was told they were out of order. i explained to the staff member that i have a health issue and required urgent use of the toilet there and then, and if i could please use the staff  bathrooms as an exception. he said he would ask to him manager. the manger then came, and i explained the situation again. she said that i could use the bathroom at pret, on the other side of the road. i've explained that it was extremely ugent and i needed to please use the staff bathroom, as a courtesy. she replied (quoting) "we don't have a staff bathroom. we also go to pret when we need the bathroom". very hard to believe. i then went to pret, on the other side of the road, when they let me use the bathroom immediately, when explaining the situation and without need of further purchase. since pret finsbury park offers much better service to both custumers and staff of starbucks finsbury park (according to their manager, staff uses bathrooms at pret!) i suggest that they all buy their drinks and seek employment there, and not at starbucks finsbury park!

incidentally, i attended starbuck caledonian road today, between 10am and 11am, and had the exact same incident (i regulary go to this starbucks and never hada problem before, to be clear) - bathroom out of order; asked to please use the staff bathroom; was told togo to the cafe at the other side of the road, that there is no staff bathroom, and claimed that staff also go elsewhere! starbucks should review its staff policy, be more transparent with custumers and definitely improve their inclusivity of people with specia needs/ chronic health conditions!

author: DorinOros
review: i come to london once a month in the office, living in manchester and working from home. today i got a request from a friend to pick up a you are here mug, but wasn’t sure if i should get one with england and/or one with london, so i bought both. later on i was able to find out that only the london mug was required, so after lunch i made my way back to the store to ask if i could return it. i got there and took my place in the queue, in front of me being two groups, one of four and one of two. the lady with curly hair, renata, seemed extremely annoyed that she was there at the till, and you could feel her negative energy in the air, it made me very uncomfortable. one person from the group of four has asked for a frappuccino, and she replied very annoyed and sarcastic “ugh, which one? we have so many!!” obviously it was a burden for her to list all of the different drinks to a new cuatomer. she took all four orders, then got to the two ladies in front of me, again, tone of voice extremely hot and bothered, which again, made everyone very uncomfortable. then my turn came. i explained to her in as much detail my mug situation and if there is a way that i could return it. i could see the disgust in her eyes when she went “i need to speak to a supervisor about it”, to which i said “yes, sure, could you call someone?” she was alone by the way. to which she replied “yeah, someone will talk to you! can you move there so i serve the ones behind you?” at this point i started to become quite annoyed. a lady with glasses came over and she took her aside and started telling her in a very condescending way about my request. then sofia came over and renata began telling her too about my request, heard her loud and clear “it’s not my fault now that he doesn’t want it”. sofia came over to me, kindly listened to what i had to say and she dealt with the issue in the most easygoing way, havind a refund issued within the minute, with no fuss and without causing a scene. to which i mentioned to sofia, thinking that she is the supervisor in question, that renata, bless her soul, i don’t know what she’s going through today, but she should not be serving customers in this fould mood she’s in. to my surprise i found out that renata is in fact, the store manager. this absolutely shocked me to the core!!!! i was a store manager for your competitor nero for a few years, so i know how stressful the job can get, but that gives you absolutely no right to take it out to the customers who provide you with your salary. i cannot imagine someone like renata even being in a hospitality position, not even speaking about store manager when her attitude is better suited on a farm with livestock. absolutely disgusted! and i’m not someone who gets disgusted quite easily, having worked in hospitality too, so i had my fair share of shocks. this today left me floored. needless to say, sofia and tonia were so sweet and kind! i don’t know why you haven’t promoted them to the position of managers, since they are 100 times more suited for the job than the sad excuse of a manager that is renata. this name will haunt me forever!! she should be going back to the farm she probably came from!!

author: Sir Khris (‘Kaptayn’)
review:
the starbucks at 11 leadenhall street, london, offers a convenient and comfortable spot for coffee and light snacks in the heart of the city of london. it's a popular location for both office workers and tourists, given its proximity to major landmarks like leadenhall market and the lloyd's building.

customers frequently commend the friendly and efficient service, with particular praise for the staff's attentiveness and ability to remember regulars' orders. the atmosphere is described as welcoming and comfortable, making it a good place for a quick coffee break or a longer work session with free wi-fi.

the quality of the beverages and food items is generally consistent with what one would expect from starbucks, offering a range of coffees, teas, pastries, and sandwiches. regulars appreciate the reliability of their favorite drinks being made to standard, and the availability of seasonal specials adds some variety throughout the year.

the store operates from 6:00 am to 6:00 pm from monday to friday, but is closed on weekends. this schedule caters well to the working crowd in the financial district. its location near major tube stations like monument and bank, as well as several bus stops, makes it easily accessible.

overall, this starbucks location is appreciated for its convenient location, friendly staff, and comfortable environment. while it might not offer anything uniquely different from other starbucks, it consistently delivers on the brand's promise of quality and service, making it a reliable choice for coffee in the city of london.

author: Aaron
review:
this is a nice starbucks location on whitechapel rd. i visited here daily during my recent trip to london, due to it being located right next to my hotel, on both occasions ordering my favourite, iced pumpkin spice latte

on both occasions, my drinks were very good, the speed of service could have been slightly better, although they can get pretty busy, and i'd rather them take a little longer to make the drinks with effort and care, which appeared to be the case here.

the interior is comfortable, and there's a good amount of seating, and a nice feel around the cafe area.

prices are in line with other starbucks locations in london, a little more expensive than the rest of the uk, but that's ok.

it would have been nice to see them remain open a little better, but there are other starbucks locations nearby, so i can understand them wanting to close some stores early.

review:
don’t go if you have allergies!

went and got a coffee, white mocha coconut milk and vegan cream, the guy serving was rushing, understandably as there was a queue but charged me £5.70 clearly heard my order tho as he said it back, as i was about to ask for receipt he just yelled next and started serving someone else, waited and got my drink which was actually a normal mocha with regular milk and regular cream, i drank some and as i have allergies, started to have a reaction, went back and told another guy who was giving out the drinks and he didn’t even apologise, even though i told him i have allergies. was kind of aggressive and rude about it even though they made the mistake, told a lady to make my drink (coconut white mocha with cream, meant to be vegan cream???) which she then asked to doublecheck my order thankfully, i did get my drink in the end, but i don’t see any reason to be rude when when you’ve made someone have a allergic reaction. definitely wouldn’t recommend if you have allergies as they do not take seriously at all.

review:
i ordered one flat white and the coffee taste was spot on. i can’t say anything about the coffee taste but the customer service, wow i didn’t see that was coming. i don’t remember his name but he’s a caucasian descent british male worker and overweight. i walked in on monday probably around 1 or 2 afternoon.

he purposely ignored my order and had a very passive aggressive attitude. the message came so clean, that he didn’t want to take me as a customer. i asked for a cup of tap water and honestly, i didn’t understand if that was too much asking for a starbucks partner but he never replied to me anything even when he passed me my coffee and water, instead of that he just put the water down forcefully. i felt so humiliated by this incident and i only could assume it was racism towards me since i’m a korean chick. i think they need to take action.

review:
strategically located across from the british museum, this starbucks remains true to itself: it does the job without surprising. the coffee here, while unpretentious, has the merit of being decent and consistent, true to the brand's standards.

however, the experience is marred by a less-than-warm welcome from the staff, which contrasts with the chain's usual ethos. furthermore, the restrooms, both upstairs and downstairs, were closed during my visit, a notable inconvenience for such a busy place.

in short, a convenient stop for a quick coffee, but nothing more.

author: Swoosy Sparkle
review:
as a regular visitor with my therapy dog, i cannot speak highly enough of the outstanding treatment we receive from the dedicated staff. each day, we are greeted with warmth and respect, making our visits truly enjoyable.

recently, we encountered an unfortunate situation where a disruptive man insisted my 'animal' be removed. the staff handled the situation with grace and professionalism, calmly explaining starbucks' dog-friendly policy. despite facing unwarranted abuse and this man taking pictures of them and me they remained composed and focused on providing exceptional service.

it's evident that the team here goes above and beyond to create a welcoming environment for all customers, including those with four-legged friends. their commitment to exemplary customer care shines through, making each visit the highlight of my day.

review:
this starbucks is nice, however the prices are top whack. they expect you to pay £5+ for a sandwich which is around the same price as a coffee, which is horrendous pricing.

we spent around £14 on three drinks, and i ordered (for myself) a mint chocolate chip frappe, my usual order whenever i go to starbucks. they got the order wrong and gave me a chocolate choc chip frappe, which i still enjoyed. you cant blame people for small mistakes like that, as they are only human and they make mistakes once in a while.

if you are looking for a drink around fulham broadway station, i would recommend going to a local corner shop and purchasing a canned drink as they are much cheaper and do the same job.

author: Helene Bautista
review: i recently ended my study abroad semester in london and visited this starbucks location regularly. i was highly impressed as an american experiencing starbucks for the first time in england. everyone here is very kind and welcoming. they offer the best customer experience through the attention to detail from coffee orders to the cleanliness of the space. they go above and beyond and are the sweetest people you’ll ever come across. i’m going to miss my daily coffee trips here and the excellent customer service. a big shout out to darren, riaz and hafsa for providing exceptional service and for being so kind and generous. this mile end starbucks team works wonderfully together and truly prioritizes the customer and quality of service. they set the bar high and upon my return i will be in search of a similar starbucks experience, but i don’t think anything will compare to the special memories i made here. i am very grateful for all the team members that work here and wish them all the best!

keep in mind high street location has limited parking spaces, so need to factor in a little extra time to park in the nearby retail park and walk to the store

review:
they really need to train the staff on the right recipes and combinations to get consistent taste. i have had to return my drink and ask it be remade multiple times.

also, says they close at 7pm but once it’s 6:30pm they say “sorry we’re closed” and you’re badgered till you chug down what you got and leave. they also will turn you away if you weren’t already in.

they start clearing up from that point, practically forcing you out. i understand they’re eager to close for the day, but then just change your advertised time to 6pm or 6:30pm instead of turning people away when your doors are still open, and making customers uncomfortable till they leave.

review: came into the new starbucks for a change. the lady serving me had stopped before giving me my change to send a txt on her phone and then the coffee frappuccino i received that i was charged 4.30 was the worst i’ve ever had it was basically a little bit of coffee and water obviously half heartedly made . won’t be coming back and will be returning to costa where the staff are attentive and the frappuccino are lovely and made how they are meant to.

most of the time the drink consistency is wrong and doesn't taste like what it is supposed to also doesnt seem like staff has much training in making these drinks paying £4.50 for a drink that doesn't taste like it should has the coffee in has always less than what it should be and the rude incompetent staff ain't it... the costa a lil further in is way better and where i have been getting my drinks

author: Nikolay Bogdev
review:
this is my second time visiting this location. the first experience was terrible - an iced coffee drink was served lukewarm and incredibly milky. thought to give them the benefit of the doubt and try again…big mistake.

ordered an iced caramel latte - received iced caramel milk with no hint of coffee. my wife asked about their seasonal drinks to which the barista looked like a deer in headlights. after giving us our drinks, the barista proceeded to sit on the counter. the same one where your food and drinks are prepared. seems like basic hygiene and etiquette are not things the management teaches.

looking at all the other reviews, it’s clear that the staff are clueless on how to make even the most basic of drinks.

i will not be returning to this starbucks as i’m sure a lot of the other reviewers won’t be. if you want to retain customers, either the staff need to be trained properly or replaced with more competent ones.

author: Abdurrahman Sertel
review:
today, as i usually do, i placed a tall oat flat white order through the mobile app. however, what i received was a small cup with an espresso and an excessive amount of foam added with a spoon. this is not the first time this has happened
i have previously returned my coffee for the same reason.

when choosing a brand known for quality coffee, i expect my order to be prepared correctly and with care. however, it seems that when the manager is not present, the coffee is not made to standard. given that i can make a much better coffee at home, i don’t think i should have to tolerate this level of inconsistency.

i kindly request that this issue be addressed and that staff be reminded of the proper way to prepare drinks.

best regards
